item.description: Spirit Airlines announced in a statement that it would wind down operations immediately, canceling all flights, over rising jet fuel costs and struggling with profitability. NBC News’ Jay Blackman reports on how the decision will impact thousands of employees and travelers.

media:content.media:title: Spirit Airlines announces all flights are canceled as it shuts down operations
media:content.media:description: Spirit Airlines announced in a statement that it would wind down operations immediately, canceling all flights, over rising jet fuel costs and struggling with profitability. NBC News’ Jay Blackman reports on how the decision will impact thousands of employees and travelers.

item.title: Spirit Airlines announces all flights are canceled as it shuts down operations

item.dc:creator: Alexandra Marquez
item.description: Danco Laboratories, one of makers of the mifepristone abortion pill on Saturday asked the Supreme Court to pause a lower court ruling that imposed a nationwide requirement for the medication to be dispensed in-person.

media:content.media:text: A hand with black fingernails holding up a box of Mifepristone abortion pills in front of the U.S. Supreme Court building.
media:content.media:description: In its filing, Danco Laboratories argued that the lower court went too far by immediately limiting the sale of mifepristone to in-person distribution

item.title: Supreme Court asked to pause ruling blocking telehealth and mail access to abortion pills

item.dc:creator: Alexandra Marquez
item.description: Danco Laboratories, one of makers of the mifepristone abortion pill on Saturday asked the Supreme Court to pause a lower court ruling that imposed a nationwide requirement for the medication to be dispensed in-person.

media:content.media:text: A hand with black fingernails holding up a box of Mifepristone abortion pills in front of the U.S. Supreme Court building.
media:content.media:description: In its filing, Danco Laboratories argued that the lower court went too far by immediately limiting the sale of mifepristone to in-person distribution

item.title: Supreme Court asked to pause ruling blocking telehealth and mail access to abortion pills

item.description: The Pentagon will withdraw roughly 5,000 U.S. troops from Germany, a move that comes amid President Trump’s growing clash with European allies over Iran and NATO tensions. NBC News’ Courtney Kube reports.

media:content.media:title: Trump administration to pull 5,000 troops from Germany
media:content.media:description: The Pentagon will withdraw roughly 5,000 U.S. troops from Germany, a move that comes amid President Trump’s growing clash with European allies over Iran and NATO tensions. NBC News’ Courtney Kube reports.

item.title: Trump administration to pull 5,000 troops from Germany

item.dc:creator: Molly Hunter
item.description: AL-MUGHAYYIR, OCCUPIED WEST BANK – A boy’s blood still stained the sidewalk in front of his school, days after an Israeli settler shot him dead

media:content.media:text: Aws al-Nasaan, a Palestinian teenager killed buy Israeli forces in the West Bank village of Al-Mughayyir.
media:content.media:description: Aws al-Nasaan, a Palestinian teenager killed buy Israeli forces in the West Bank village of Al-Mughayyir.

media:content.media:text: Aws al-Nasaan is carried after being shot in the West Bank village of Al-Mughayyir. A pool of blood lies on the floor.
media:content.media:description: Aws al-Nasaan is carried after being shot in the West Bank village of Al-Mughayyir. A pool of blood lies on the floor.

media:content.media:credit: Supplied to NBC News; Mahmoud Illean / AP
media:content.media:text: Left: An undated photo of Aws al-Nasaan with his younger brother Kareem. Right: Kareem mourns his older brother in the West Bank village of al-Mughayyir on April 22.
media:content.media:description: Left: An undated photo of Aws al-Nasaan with his younger brother Kareem. Right: Kareem mourns his older brother in the West Bank village of al-Mughayyir on April 22.

media:content.media:text: Some 3,200 Palestinians from dozens of Bedouin and herding communities have been forced from their homes by settler violence and movement restrictions since October 2023, the UN's humanitarian agency OCHA reported in October. All Israeli settlements in the West Bank are considered illegal by the international community.
media:content.media:description: Children play near the West Bank village of Jalud, south of Nablus, on April 19, with the Israeli settlement of Ahiya seen in the background.
People clash with Israeli border police during the funeral of 14-year-old Aws al-Nasaan in the West Bank village of al-Mughayyir, on April 22.

item.title: Boy, 14, shot dead by Israeli settlers in West Bank amid escalation in violence

item.dc:creator: Jeremy Mikula
Joe Murphy
item.description: James Comey gets indicted again, the Pentagon says how much the Iran war has cost taxpayers, and viral trend is removing door handles.
item.title: King Charles III cracks some jokes, and a congressional map gets thrown out: The news quiz

item.description: Man climbs D.C. bridge to protest Iran war

media:content.media:title: Man climbs D.C. bridge to protest Iran war
media:content.media:description: Man climbs D.C. bridge to protest Iran war

item.title: Man climbs D.C. bridge to protest Iran war

item.description: NBC News' Ryan Nobles joins "Here's the Scoop" to report on contradictory statements from Republicans on the state of the Iran war and how that messaging could affect midterm elections.

media:content.media:title: How Republican messaging on Iran war could affect midterms
media:content.media:description: NBC News' Ryan Nobles joins "Here's the Scoop" to report on contradictory statements from Republicans on the state of the Iran war and how that messaging could affect midterm elections.

item.title: How Republican messaging on Iran war could affect midterms

media:content.media:description: Spirit Airlines could shut down without bailout

item.title: Spirit Airlines could shut down without bailout

item.description: Trump signs bill ending partial government shutdown

media:content.media:description: Trump signs bill ending partial government shutdown

item.title: Trump signs bill ending partial government shutdown

item.description: As album rollouts continue to evolve, fans recently attempted to melt a giant ice block in the middle of Toronto as part of Drake's "Iceman" announcement. But how did we get here? NBC News' Gadi Schwartz takes a look back at other memorable rollouts, and how they have advanced through the years.

media:content.media:title: The evolution of album rollouts
media:content.media:description: As album rollouts continue to evolve, fans recently attempted to melt a giant ice block in the middle of Toronto as part of Drake's "Iceman" announcement. But how did we get here? NBC News' Gadi Schwartz takes a look back at other memorable rollouts, and how they have advanced through the years.

item.title: The evolution of album rollouts

item.dc:creator: Alexandra Marquez
item.description: Danco Laboratories, one of makers of the mifepristone abortion pill on Saturday asked the Supreme Court to pause a lower court ruling that imposed a nationwide requirement for the medication to be dispensed in-person.

media:content.media:text: A hand with black fingernails holding up a box of Mifepristone abortion pills in front of the U.S. Supreme Court building.
media:content.media:description: In its filing, Danco Laboratories argued that the lower court went too far by immediately limiting the sale of mifepristone to in-person distribution

item.title: Supreme Court asked to pause ruling blocking telehealth and mail access to abortion pills

item.dc:creator: Yuliya Talmazan
item.description: European leaders called for the continent to learn how to defend itself Saturday after Pentagon officials said the U.S. was withdrawing approximately 5,000 troops from Germany

media:content.media:description: U.S. Army soldiers in Hohenfels, Germany, on April 30, 2026.

item.title: Europe rattled by 'disastrous trend' as Trump pulls 5,000 troops out of Germany

item.dc:creator: Molly Hunter
item.description: AL-MUGHAYYIR, OCCUPIED WEST BANK – A boy’s blood still stained the sidewalk in front of his school, days after an Israeli settler shot him dead

media:content.media:text: Aws al-Nasaan, a Palestinian teenager killed buy Israeli forces in the West Bank village of Al-Mughayyir.
media:content.media:description: Aws al-Nasaan, a Palestinian teenager killed buy Israeli forces in the West Bank village of Al-Mughayyir.

media:content.media:text: Aws al-Nasaan is carried after being shot in the West Bank village of Al-Mughayyir. A pool of blood lies on the floor.
media:content.media:description: Aws al-Nasaan is carried after being shot in the West Bank village of Al-Mughayyir. A pool of blood lies on the floor.

media:content.media:credit: Supplied to NBC News; Mahmoud Illean / AP
media:content.media:text: Left: An undated photo of Aws al-Nasaan with his younger brother Kareem. Right: Kareem mourns his older brother in the West Bank village of al-Mughayyir on April 22.
media:content.media:description: Left: An undated photo of Aws al-Nasaan with his younger brother Kareem. Right: Kareem mourns his older brother in the West Bank village of al-Mughayyir on April 22.

media:content.media:text: Some 3,200 Palestinians from dozens of Bedouin and herding communities have been forced from their homes by settler violence and movement restrictions since October 2023, the UN's humanitarian agency OCHA reported in October. All Israeli settlements in the West Bank are considered illegal by the international community.
media:content.media:description: Children play near the West Bank village of Jalud, south of Nablus, on April 19, with the Israeli settlement of Ahiya seen in the background.
People clash with Israeli border police during the funeral of 14-year-old Aws al-Nasaan in the West Bank village of al-Mughayyir, on April 22.

item.title: Boy, 14, shot dead by Israeli settlers in West Bank amid escalation in violence

item.dc:creator: Aria Bendix
item.description: An artificial intelligence model from the Mayo Clinic detected early signs of possible pancreatic cancer on scans up to two years before patients were diagnosed.

media:content.media:text: The REDMOD AI model analyzes a CT scan to detect early tissue changes associated with pancreatic cancer.
media:content.media:description: The Mayo Clinic's AI model analyzes a CT scan to detect early tissue changes associated with pancreatic cancer.

item.title: AI finds signs of pancreatic cancer before tumors develop
